Chaim Weizmann.event: Born in Motal, Russian Empire (today Belarus)
Moved to Zurich, Switzerland for doctoral studies
Received PhD in chemistry from University of Fribourg, Switzerland
Moved to Manchester, England as lecturer in chemistry
Became prominent leader in the British Zionist Federation
Played key role in securing the Balfour Declaration from the British government
Elected President of the World Zionist Organization at the Carlsbad Congress
Resigned as WZO President after internal disputes
Re-elected President of the World Zionist Organization
Led Zionist opposition to the British White Paper restricting immigration to Palestine
Chaired the Biltmore Conference in New York, calling for a Jewish Commonwealth
Lobbied at the UN for partition of Palestine
Elected as the first President of the State of Israel
Died in Rehovot, Israel; buried at Weizmann Institute of Science

Sir Moses Haim Montefiore.event: Born in Livorno, Tuscany, Italy, to a Sephardic Jewish family.
Became broker at London Stock Exchange.
Married Judith Cohen; linked to Rothschild family.
First visit to Jerusalem; became strictly observant Jew.
Purchased East Cliff Lodge, Ramsgate; later built Montefiore Synagogue (1833).
Elected President of the Board of Deputies of British Jews.
Elected Sheriff of the City of London; knighted by Queen Victoria.
Mission to Damascus to intervene in the Damascus Affair blood libel case.
Met Tsar Nicholas I in Russia to intercede for Jews; awarded baronetcy the same year.
Established agricultural training project near Jaffa using Judah Touro’s bequest.
Advocated in Rome for release of Jewish boy Edgardo Mortara.
Founded Mishkenot Sha’ananim, first Jewish neighborhood outside Jerusalem’s walls.
Mission to Safi, Morocco, against blood libel accusations.
Second mission to Russia; met Tsar Alexander II.
Celebrated 100th birthday with major public celebrations in Ramsgate.
Died in Ramsgate at age 100; buried beside wife Judith in Montefiore Mausoleum.

Solomon Schechter.event: Studied at yeshiva in Piatra Neamț
Studied under Rabbi Joseph Saul Nathanson in Lemberg
Studied at Rabbinical College in Vienna under Meir Friedmann
Studied at Berlin Hochschule and University of Berlin
Invited to Britain to tutor rabbinics under Claude Montefiore in London
Appointed lecturer in Talmudics and reader in Rabbinics at Cambridge University
Excavated the Cairo Geniza with support from Hermann Adler and Aaron Raphael Ben Shim'on
Professor of Hebrew at University College London
Became President of the Jewish Theological Seminary of America in New York
Died and buried at Mount Hebron Cemetery, Queens, New York

Theodor Herzl.event: Studied law at the University of Vienna
Worked as Paris correspondent for the Neue Freie Presse
Organized the First Zionist Congress in Basel, Switzerland
Died in Edlach, near Reichenau an der Rax, Austria; initially buried in Vienna, later reinterred in Jerusalem
